ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza food product class (FOODON_00002572). No specific white pizza (pizza bianca) subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific white pizza variant code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 covers pizza generically. No white pizza subtype exists in SNOMED CT; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Italia white pizza weighing approximately 300 g, as typically served at an Italian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza, cheese, regular crust (FDC ID 170720) and Pizza with meat, regular crust (FDC ID 170721), adapted for white pizza with mozzarella, parma ham and mushroom
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for pizza bianca (white pizza without tomato sauce) with mozzarella, parma ham and mushroom. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza (FDC ID 170720) as the closest defensible analogue, with minor upward adjustment to protein and sodium to account for parma ham, and slight reduction in carbohydrate to reflect absence of tomato sauce. Mushroom contribution is nutritionally minor at typical topping quantities. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; pizza, cheese, regular crust (GI ~60)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pizza bianca (white pizza) with mozzarella, parma ham and mushroom. The closest approved GI source entry is cheese pizza on regular crust (GI approximately 60, medium), from Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values published in Diabetes Care, as indexed in the University of Sydney GI Database. White pizza without tomato sauce has a similar carbohydrate base and fat-protein profile; GI 60 is used as the analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 90.6) / 100 = 54.4, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 30.2 × 3 = 90.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~1860 mg per whole pizza serving) driven by mozzarella cheese and parma ham
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from cured parma ham and mozzarella; a single serving may exceed 80% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) and high glycemic load per serving (~54) from refined wheat pizza base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions due to its medium GI and high per-serving glycemic load; pairing with a salad and limiting portion to one or two slices can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mozzarella (~4.8 g per 100 g) and sodium from parma ham contribute to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this pizza due to its combined saturated fat and sodium burden; occasional consumption of one to two slices as part of an otherwise heart-healthy diet is preferable to regular full-pizza servings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Italia White Pizza is an Italian-style pizza bianca (white pizza) topped with mozzarella cheese, thinly sliced parma ham (prosciutto di Parma), and mushrooms, baked on a regular wheat-flour crust without tomato sauce. The absence of tomato sauce distinguishes it from conventional red-sauce pizzas; instead, the base is typically brushed with olive oil and garlic, allowing the creamy mozzarella and the delicate saltiness of the parma ham to dominate the flavour profile. The crust is crisp on the exterior and chewy within, while the mozzarella melts to a soft, slightly elastic layer. Mushrooms add an earthy umami note. Nutritionally, this pizza provides approximately 266 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (~12.5 g) from cheese and ham, moderate fat (~10.8 g, of which ~4.8 g saturated), and refined carbohydrate (~30.2 g) from the wheat base. Sodium content is high (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the cured ham and cheese, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), and a full 300 g serving carries a high glycemic load (~54), relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The saturated fat and sodium profile also warrants caution for cardiovascular disease. In Singapore, this dish is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and is positioned as a premium sharing or individual main course item.

Is white pizza healthier than regular pizza?
Mozzarella parma ham mushroom pizza nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for pizza bianca with mozzarella, parma ham and mushroom was found in any primary approved source. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza (FDC ID 170720) as the closest defensible analogue, with adjustments for parma ham and absence of tomato sauce. GI is estimated from the closest approved GI database entry (cheese pizza, regular crust, GI ~60). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza source entry used as the analogue basis. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza data and are plausible for this food type. All -1 sentinel values reflect genuine absence of reporting in the adapted source, not confirmed zero values. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are nutritionally present and non-zero for this baked wheat-cheese-meat pizza. GI source is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for pizza bianca with parma ham and mushroom was located. Multilingual names are provided for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi in accordance with the Singapore serving context. This food is served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore - Boat Quay via field survey data (price SGD 30.41). The food_id GD002997 is preserved from the input seed record.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutrient profile adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza as closest approved analogue.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes.
Set GI to 60 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate; documented estimation basis.
Provided multilingual names in Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singapore restaurant context.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Confirmed all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none).
Confirmed gi_category 'medium' is correct for GI=60 (56-69 range).
Confirmed all core macros are non-zero with no zero-justification phrase needed.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name exactly 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
